title: Customer service email
description: Empathetic but efficient customer reply at high speed
text: Hi Mark, thank you for reaching out, and I'm sorry to hear about the trouble with order #8432. I've checked the shipment, and it appears the parcel was delayed at the regional hub. A replacement is now on its way and should arrive by Friday. Please let me know if there's anything else I can help with. Best regards, Lisa.

tips.text: A support desk answers dozens of these a day. The reader on the other end wants two things: to be heard, and to be helped.
Warmth and speed are not at odds. A reply that lands fast and reads kindly is the whole craft here.

tips.target: Hi Mark, thank you for reaching out, and I'm sorry to hear about the trouble with order #8432. I've checked the shipment, and it appears the parcel was delayed at

tips.text: A phrase like "thank you for reaching out" should arrive without thought, so your attention stays on the customer's actual problem.

tips.target: the regional hub. A replacement is now on its way and should arrive by Friday. Please let me know if there's anything else I can help with. Best regards, Lisa.

character_tips.anna: Support is high-volume, real-world typing. Find the even rhythm and the day stops feeling like a sprint.
character_tips.maxim: A good reply is clear before it is fast. Cut the apology to one honest line, then get to the fix.
character_tips.knopych: The customer is waiting. Steady hands answer sooner than anxious ones.
character_tips.klavochka: Kindness has a rhythm too. Type the warm words as smoothly as the rest. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
